title: The Quiet Authority of the Uniform
subtitle: Why the most photographed people of the season wear the same thing every day
metaDescription: The uniform dressing movement and what it signals.
heroImageAlt: Fashion mannequin with draped terracotta and cream fabric

chapters.title: Repetition as signature
chapters.bodyHtml:
The uniform was workwear, then it was rebellion, and now it is the most legible status signal on the street: the person who has decided. A single silhouette, repeated daily, photographs like a logo and reads like a manifesto against the algorithmic churn of micro-trends.

A uniform is not the absence of choice. It is one choice, made permanently.

The houses have noticed. This season's strongest collections were not collections at all but systems: five pieces, one palette, cut to be worn in any order and photographed from any angle.

keyPoints.text: Uniform dressing reads as decision, not deprivation.
Repetition photographs like identity and resists trend churn.
Collections are becoming systems of interchangeable pieces.
